## 5.2 PART A: Short Answer Questions (3 Marks Each)

> **[KTU University Exam - Dec 2024]**
> **Q1.** Explain the difference between `LinearLayout`, `RelativeLayout`, and `GridLayout` in Android with a suitable example for each.

**Model Answer (3 Marks):**

> [!NOTE]
> **Definition (1 Mark)**: A layout in Android defines the visual structure of a UI. `LinearLayout` arranges children in a single row or column. `RelativeLayout` positions children relative to parent or siblings. `GridLayout` places children in a rectangular grid of cells.

**Key Differences Table (1 Mark):**

| Layout | Positioning | Best For |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| LinearLayout | Sequential | Simple forms, lists |
| RelativeLayout | Constraint-based | Complex overlapping UIs |
| GridLayout | Cell-based | Tabular forms, calculator |

**Example (1 Mark)**: For a registration form (Project 1), `LinearLayout` with `android:orientation="vertical"` is most suitable as it stacks fields in order. A `GridLayout` with `columnCount="2"` would place label and input in adjacent columns.

---

> **[KTU University Exam - May 2024]**
> **Q2.** What is an Intent in Android? Differentiate between explicit and implicit intents with one example each.

**Model Answer (3 Marks):**

**Definition (1 Mark)**: An Intent in Android is a messaging object used to request an action from another app component. It is a passive data structure holding an abstract description of an operation to be performed.

**Explicit Intent (1 Mark)**: Specifies the target component by class name. Used for in-app navigation.
```java
Intent i = new Intent(CurrentActivity.this, SecondActivity.class);
startActivity(i);
```

**Implicit Intent (1 Mark)**: Specifies an action, and the system finds a suitable app.
```java
Intent i = new Intent(Intent.ACTION_DIAL, Uri.parse("tel:9876543210"));
startActivity(i);
```

---

> **[KTU University Exam - Dec 2023]**
> **Q3.** What is the role of `SQLiteOpenHelper` in Android? List its two key methods.

**Model Answer (3 Marks):**

**Role (2 Marks)**: `SQLiteOpenHelper` is an abstract class that manages database creation and version management. It encapsulates the boilerplate code for opening, creating, and upgrading SQLite databases. It provides `getReadableDatabase()` and `getWritableDatabase()` methods to obtain `SQLiteDatabase` instances.

**Two Key Methods (1 Mark):**
1. `onCreate(SQLiteDatabase db)`: Called when the database is created for the first time. Typically contains `CREATE TABLE` statements.
2. `onUpgrade(SQLiteDatabase db, int oldVersion, int newVersion)`: Called when the database version is incremented. Used to handle schema migrations.

---

> **[KTU University Exam - May 2023]**
> **Q4.** Explain the Fragment lifecycle. Why is it useful for adaptive UI?

**Model Answer (3 Marks):**

**Lifecycle Methods (2 Marks):** `onAttach() ‚Üí onCreate() ‚Üí onCreateView() ‚Üí onActivityCreated() ‚Üí onStart() ‚Üí onResume() ‚Üí onPause() ‚Üí onStop() ‚Üí onDestroyView() ‚Üí onDetach()`.

**Usefulness (1 Mark):** Fragments enable **adaptive UI** design. For example, in Project 2, the same app shows a list-detail view as two separate screens in portrait, and as side-by-side panels in landscape ‚Äî without duplicating business logic.

---

> **[KTU University Exam - Dec 2022]**
> **Q5.** Compare `SharedPreferences` and `SQLite` for local data storage in Android.

**Model Answer (3 Marks):**

| Feature | SharedPreferences | SQLite |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Data Type** | Key-value pairs (primitives) | Complex relational data |
| **Capacity** | Small (settings, flags) | Large (thousands of rows) |
| **API** | `getSharedPreferences()` | `SQLiteOpenHelper` |
| **Best For** | User prefs (Project 10) | User records (Projects 6,7,8) |
| **Storage** | XML file | DB file |

## 5.3 PART B: Long Answer Questions (14 Marks Each ‚Äî Internal Choice)

### Question A (14 Marks) ‚Äî Project 2 Focused

> **[KTU University Exam - May 2024]**
> **Q6A.** Develop an Android application that displays a list of candidate names. On selecting a name, the details of the candidate should be shown on the next screen with a "Back" button. When the device is rotated to landscape mode, the application should display the list on the left fragment and the details on the right fragment, without using a second activity. Use Fragment transactions and a configuration change listener.

**Model Solution (14 Marks):**

**Part (a): Basic List-Detail with Two Activities/Fragments (7 Marks)**

**Step 1: Create `Candidate` model class (1 Mark)**
```java
public class Candidate {
    String name, age, qualification;
    public Candidate(String name, String age, String qualification) {
        this.name = name;
        this.age = age;
        this.qualification = qualification;
    }
    // Getters
}
```

**Step 2: Create `ListFragment.java` (2 Marks)**
```java
public class ListFragment extends Fragment {
    private ListView listView;
    private List<Candidate> candidates;
    
    @Nullable
    @Override
    public View onCreateView(@NonNull LayoutInflater inflater, 
                             @Nullable ViewGroup container, 
                             @Nullable Bundle savedInstanceState) {
        View view = inflater.inflate(R.layout.fragment_list, container, false);
        listView = view.findViewById(R.id.listView);
        
        // Populate sample data
        candidates = new ArrayList<>();
        candidates.add(new Candidate("Alice", "25", "BTech"));
        candidates.add(new Candidate("Bob", "28", "MTech"));
        candidates.add(new Candidate("Charlie", "22", "BCA"));
        
        ArrayAdapter<String> adapter = new ArrayAdapter<>(
            getActivity(), android.R.layout.simple_list_item_1,
            candidates.stream().map(Candidate::getName).collect(Collectors.toList())
        );
        listView.setAdapter(adapter);
        
        listView.setOnItemClickListener((parent, view1, position, id) -> {
            Candidate selected = candidates.get(position);
            Bundle bundle = new Bundle();
            bundle.putString("name", selected.getName());
            bundle.putString("age", selected.getAge());
            bundle.putString("qual", selected.getQualification());
            
            DetailsFragment detailsFragment = new DetailsFragment();
            detailsFragment.setArguments(bundle);
            
            // Check orientation
            if (getResources().getConfiguration().orientation == 
                    Configuration.ORIENTATION_PORTRAIT) {
                getFragmentManager().beginTransaction()
                    .replace(R.id.fragment_container, detailsFragment)
                    .addToBackStack(null)
                    .commit();
            } else {
                getFragmentManager().beginTransaction()
                    .replace(R.id.fragment_container_right, detailsFragment)
                    .commit();
            }
        });
        return view;
    }
}
```

**Step 3: Create `DetailsFragment.java` (1 Mark)**
```java
public class DetailsFragment extends Fragment {
    @Nullable
    @Override
    public View onCreateView(@NonNull LayoutInflater inflater, 
                             @Nullable ViewGroup container, 
                             @Nullable Bundle savedInstanceState) {
        View view = inflater.inflate(R.layout.fragment_details, container, false);
        TextView tvName = view.findViewById(R.id.tvName);
        TextView tvAge = view.findViewById(R.id.tvAge);
        TextView tvQual = view.findViewById(R.id.tvQual);
        Button btnBack = view.findViewById(R.id.btnBack);
        
        Bundle args = getArguments();
        if (args != null) {
            tvName.setText("Name: " + args.getString("name"));
            tvAge.setText("Age: " + args.getString("age"));
            tvQual.setText("Qualification: " + args.getString("qual"));
        }
        
        btnBack.setOnClickListener(v -> {
            if (getFragmentManager().getBackStackEntryCount() > 0) {
                getFragmentManager().popBackStack();
            }
        });
        return view;
    }
}
```

**Step 4: `MainActivity.java` with config listener (2 Marks)**
```java
public class MainActivity extends AppCompatActivity {
    @Override
    protected void onCreate(Bundle savedInstanceState) {
        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState);
        setContentView(R.layout.activity_main);
        
        if (savedInstanceState == null) {
            getSupportFragmentManager().beginTransaction()
                .replace(R.id.fragment_container, new ListFragment())
                .commit();
        }
    }
    
    @Override
    public void onConfigurationChanged(Configuration newConfig) {
        super.onConfigurationChanged(newConfig);
        if (newConfig.orientation == Configuration.ORIENTATION_LANDSCAPE) {
            // Landscape: ListFragment in left, DetailsFragment in right
            Toast.makeText(this, "Landscape mode", Toast.LENGTH_SHORT).show();
        } else if (newConfig.orientation == Configuration.ORIENTATION_PORTRAIT) {
            // Portrait: Just listFragment, details via separate fragment
            Toast.makeText(this, "Portrait mode", Toast.LENGTH_SHORT).show();
        }
    }
}
```

**Step 5: AndroidManifest.xml configChanges (1 Mark)**
```xml
<activity android:name=".MainActivity"
    android:configChanges="orientation|screenSize|keyboardHidden">
</activity>
```

**Part (b): Explanation of Fragment Transactions and Rotation Event Listener (7 Marks)**

**Fragment Transactions (3 Marks):**
- `FragmentManager` manages Fragments in an Activity.
- `beginTransaction()` starts a transaction.
- `replace(int containerViewId, Fragment fragment)` replaces the current Fragment in a container.
- `addToBackStack(String name)` adds the transaction to the back stack, allowing the user to reverse the transaction with the Back button.
- `commit()` executes the transaction.

**Rotation Event Listener (2 Marks):**
- Two approaches: (1) Let Activity recreate and save state via `onSaveInstanceState()`. (2) Handle manually via `onConfigurationChanged()` and declare `configChanges` in Manifest.
- In approach 1, layout resources are reloaded with new orientation-specific resources from `res/layout-land/`.
- In approach 2, the Activity is NOT recreated, so the developer manually swaps Fragments.

**Layout for Landscape (2 Marks):**
Create `res/layout-land/activity_main.xml` with two `FrameLayout` containers side by side:
```xml
<LinearLayout android:orientation="horizontal">
    <FrameLayout android:id="@+id/fragment_container" 
                 android:layout_width="0dp" 
                 android:layout_weight="1" 
                 android:layout_height="match_parent" />
    <FrameLayout android:id="@+id/fragment_container_right" 
                 android:layout_width="0dp" 
                 android:layout_weight="1" 
                 android:layout_height="match_parent" />
</LinearLayout>
```

### Question B (14 Marks) ‚Äî Projects 6, 7, 8 Focused

> **[KTU University Exam - Dec 2024]**
> **Q6B.** Design and implement an Android application for user management with the following features:
> (a) **User Registration** screen that stores user details (username, password, email, age) in a SQLite database table called `users`. (7 Marks)
> (b) **Admin Panel** screen that lists all registered users in a `ListView`. The admin can click any record to edit the username and password. Changes must be reflected in the database. (7 Marks)

**Model Solution (14 Marks):**

**Part (a): User Registration (7 Marks)**

**Step 1: `DBHelper.java` (2 Marks)**
```java
public class DBHelper extends SQLiteOpenHelper {
    private static final String DB_NAME = "UserDB.db";
    private static final int DB_VERSION = 1;
    public static final String TABLE = "users";
    public static final String COL_ID = "id";
    public static final String COL_USER = "username";
    public static final String COL_PASS = "password";
    public static final String COL_EMAIL = "email";
    public static final String COL_AGE = "age";

    public DBHelper(Context context) {
        super(context, DB_NAME, null, DB_VERSION);
    }

    @Override
    public void onCreate(SQLiteDatabase db) {
        String sql = "CREATE TABLE " + TABLE + " (" +
                COL_ID + " INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, " +
                COL_USER + " TEXT, " +
                COL_PASS + " TEXT, " +
                COL_EMAIL + " TEXT, " +
                COL_AGE + " INTEGER)";
        db.execSQL(sql);
    }

    @Override
    public void onUpgrade(SQLiteDatabase db, int oldV, int newV) {
        db.execSQL("DROP TABLE IF EXISTS " + TABLE);
        onCreate(db);
    }
}
```

**Step 2: `RegisterActivity.java` Insert Logic (3 Marks)**
```java
btnRegister.setOnClickListener(v -> {
    String user = etUser.getText().toString();
    String pass = etPass.getText().toString();
    String email = etEmail.getText().toString();
    int age = Integer.parseInt(etAge.getText().toString());
    
    DBHelper dbHelper = new DBHelper(this);
    SQLiteDatabase db = dbHelper.getWritableDatabase();
    
    ContentValues values = new ContentValues();
    values.put(DBHelper.COL_USER, user);
    values.put(DBHelper.COL_PASS, pass);
    values.put(DBHelper.COL_EMAIL, email);
    values.put(DBHelper.COL_AGE, age);
    
    long result = db.insert(DBHelper.TABLE, null, values);
    
    if (result != -1) {
        Toast.makeText(this, "Registration Successful", 
            Toast.LENGTH_SHORT).show();
        // Navigate to admin
    } else {
        Toast.makeText(this, "Registration Failed", 
            Toast.LENGTH_SHORT).show();
    }
    db.close();
});
```

**Step 3: Activity Layout (1 Mark)**
```xml
<LinearLayout android:orientation="vertical" ...>
    <EditText android:id="@+id/etUser" android:hint="Username" />
    <EditText android:id="@+id/etPass" android:hint="Password" 
              android:inputType="textPassword" />
    <EditText android:id="@+id/etEmail" android:hint="Email" 
              android:inputType="textEmailAddress" />
    <EditText android:id="@+id/etAge" android:hint="Age" 
              android:inputType="number" />
    <Button android:id="@+id/btnRegister" android:text="Register" />
</LinearLayout>
```

**Step 4: Explanation (1 Mark):**
`ContentValues` is a key-value map used to insert data. `db.insert()` returns the row ID of the newly inserted row, or `-1` on error. The `SQLiteOpenHelper` pattern ensures schema version management.

**Part (b): Admin Panel with Edit (7 Marks)**

**Step 1: `AdminActivity.java` List All Users (2 Marks)**
```java
public class AdminActivity extends AppCompatActivity {
    private ListView listView;
    private SimpleCursorAdapter adapter;
    private DBHelper dbHelper;

    @Override
    protected void onCreate(Bundle savedInstanceState) {
        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState);
        setContentView(R.layout.activity_admin);
        listView = findViewById(R.id.listView);
        dbHelper = new DBHelper(this);
        loadUsers();
    }

    private void loadUsers() {
        SQLiteDatabase db = dbHelper.getReadableDatabase();
        Cursor cursor = db.query(DBHelper.TABLE, null, null, null, 
            null, null, null);
        
        String[] from = {DBHelper.COL_USER, DBHelper.COL_EMAIL};
        int[] to = {android.R.id.text1, android.R.id.text2};
        adapter = new SimpleCursorAdapter(this,
            android.R.layout.simple_list_item_2, cursor, from, to, 0);
        listView.setAdapter(adapter);
    }
}
```

**Step 2: OnClick Listener for Edit (3 Marks)**
```java
listView.setOnItemClickListener((parent, view, position, id) -> {
    Cursor cursor = (Cursor) parent.getItemAtPosition(position);
    int userId = cursor.getInt(cursor.getColumnIndexOrThrow(DBHelper.COL_ID));
    String currentUser = cursor.getString(cursor.getColumnIndexOrThrow(DBHelper.COL_USER));
    String currentPass = cursor.getString(cursor.getColumnIndexOrThrow(DBHelper.COL_PASS));
    
    // Inflate custom dialog layout
    View dialogView = LayoutInflater.from(this).inflate(R.layout.dialog_edit, null);
    EditText etEditUser = dialogView.findViewById(R.id.etEditUser);
    EditText etEditPass = dialogView.findViewById(R.id.etEditPass);
    etEditUser.setText(currentUser);
    etEditPass.setText(currentPass);
    
    new AlertDialog.Builder(this)
        .setTitle("Edit User")
        .setView(dialogView)
        .setPositiveButton("Save", (dialog, which) -> {
            String newUser = etEditUser.getText().toString();
            String newPass = etEditPass.getText().toString();
            
            SQLiteDatabase db = dbHelper.getWritableDatabase();
            ContentValues values = new ContentValues();
            values.put(DBHelper.COL_USER, newUser);
            values.put(DBHelper.COL_PASS, newPass);
            
            int rows = db.update(DBHelper.TABLE, values, 
                DBHelper.COL_ID + " = ?", 
                new String[]{String.valueOf(userId)});
            
            if (rows > 0) {
                Toast.makeText(this, "Updated successfully", 
                    Toast.LENGTH_SHORT).show();
                loadUsers(); // Refresh the list
            }
        })
        .setNegativeButton("Cancel", null)
        .show();
});
```

**Step 3: Explanation of Update Operation (2 Marks):**
- `db.update(table, ContentValues, whereClause, whereArgs)` modifies existing rows.
- The `whereClause` uses `?` placeholders that are bound by `whereArgs` array ‚Äî this prevents SQL injection.
- After updating, the cursor must be re-queried (or the Activity reloaded) to reflect the new data in the `ListView`.
- `SimpleCursorAdapter` requires the cursor to be re-queried. The `changeCursor()` method on the adapter is an efficient way to swap cursors without recreating the adapter.

## 5.4 KTU Examiner's Valuation Warning & Common Pitfalls

> [!WARNING]
> **Pitfall 1 (Layouts - Project 1)**: Students often use **nested `LinearLayout` inside `ScrollView`** incorrectly without specifying `layout_height="wrap_content"`. This causes the inner layout to not scroll properly. Always set `android:layout_height="wrap_content"` on the inner layout inside `ScrollView`.

> [!WARNING]
> **Pitfall 2 (Fragments - Project 2)**: A common mistake is to **not use `addToBackStack(null)`** in the Fragment transaction. Without this, the Back button does not return to the list. Always call `addToBackStack()` BEFORE `commit()`.

> [!WARNING]
> **Pitfall 3 (Intents - Project 3)**: Using `ACTION_CALL` instead of `ACTION_DIAL`. `ACTION_CALL` requires the `CALL_PHONE` permission at runtime, while `ACTION_DIAL` does not. **KTU expects `ACTION_DIAL`** in the question.

> [!WARNING]
> **Pitfall 4 (Notifications - Project 4)**: On API 26+ (Android 8.0 Oreo and above), notifications MUST be posted to a `NotificationChannel`. Forgetting to create the channel causes notifications to be silently dropped. **Always check `Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= Build.VERSION_CODES.O`**.

> [!WARNING]
> **Pitfall 5 (Database - Projects 6-8)**: Forgetting to **close the database** with `db.close()` leads to memory leaks. Also, students often use `db.execSQL("INSERT...")` with string concatenation, causing SQL injection vulnerabilities. **Always use `ContentValues` and parameterized queries** with `?` placeholders.

> [!WARNING]
> **Pitfall 6 (Alarm - Project 11)**: On Android 12+ (API 31+), exact alarms require the `SCHEDULE_EXACT_ALARM` permission. The app must request this via `am.canScheduleExactAlarms()` and an intent to settings. **KTU questions on alarms MUST mention this**.

> [!WARNING]
> **Pitfall 7 (Location - Projects 13/14)**: Forgetting runtime permission check leads to `SecurityException` crashes. Always wrap `fusedLocationClient.requestLocationUpdates()` in a `if (ContextCompat.checkSelfPermission(...))` block.

## 5.5 Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember

> [!NOTE]
> **High-Density Revision Checklist for KTU 2024 Exam**

### **Project 1: Layouts**
- [ ] LinearLayout: `orientation="vertical"` or `"horizontal"`.
- [ ] RelativeLayout: Use `layout_below`, `layout_toRightOf`.
- [ ] GridLayout: `columnCount="2"`, `layout_column="0"`, `layout_columnSpan="2"`.
- [ ] DatePickerDialog requires `Calendar` instance with year/month/day.
- [ ] Spinner needs `android:entries="@array/array_name"` OR a custom `ArrayAdapter`.

### **Project 2: Fragments**
- [ ] Lifecycle: `onAttach ‚Üí onCreate ‚Üí onCreateView ‚Üí onActivityCreated ‚Üí onStart ‚Üí onResume`.
- [ ] `FragmentManager.beginTransaction().replace().addToBackStack().commit()`.
- [ ] `configChanges` in Manifest to handle rotation manually.
- [ ] Use `res/layout-land/` folder for landscape-specific layouts.

### **Projects 3 & 12: Intents**
- [ ] `ACTION_DIAL` (no permission), `ACTION_VIEW` (http/https), `ACTION_SENDTO` (smsto:).
- [ ] `Uri.parse("tel:1234567890")` is mandatory.
- [ ] `onOptionsItemSelected` handles menu clicks.
- [ ] `onCreateOptionsMenu` inflates the menu XML.

### **Project 4: Notifications**
- [ ] `NotificationChannel` is mandatory for API 26+.
- [ ] `NotificationCompat.Builder` for backward compatibility.
- [ ] `setSmallIcon` (NOT `setIcon`) is required.
- [ ] `setAutoCancel(true)` dismisses on tap.

### **Project 5: File I/O**
- [ ] `openFileOutput("name", MODE_PRIVATE)` returns `FileOutputStream`.
- [ ] Split lines with `String.split("\t")`.
- [ ] Use `BufferedReader` for line-by-line reading.

### **Projects 6, 7, 8: SQLite**
- [ ] `SQLiteOpenHelper` with `onCreate` and `onUpgrade`.
- [ ] `ContentValues` for insert/update (NEVER string concatenation).
- [ ] `Cursor` for queries, `moveToNext()` for iteration.
- [ ] `db.update(table, values, "id=?", new String[]{id})` for update.
- [ ] `db.delete(table, "id=?", new String[]{id})` for delete.

### **Project 9: Content Providers**
- [ ] `READ_CONTACTS` permission required.
- [ ] `ContactsContract.Contacts.CONTENT_URI` for names.
- [ ] `ContactsContract.CommonDataKinds.Phone.CONTENT_URI` for numbers.
- [ ] `getContentResolver().query()` returns `Cursor`.

### **Project 10: SharedPreferences**
- [ ] `getSharedPreferences("name", MODE_PRIVATE)`.
- [ ] `editor.putString/putInt/putBoolean`, then `editor.apply()`.
- [ ] `apply()` is async; `commit()` is sync.

### **Project 11: AlarmManager**
- [ ] `setRepeating(AlarmManager.RTC_WAKEUP, triggerTime, interval, pendingIntent)`.
- [ ] `BroadcastReceiver` extends `onReceive(Context, Intent)`.
- [ ] `PendingIntent.FLAG_UPDATE_CURRENT` and `FLAG_IMMUTABLE` for API 31+.

### **Projects 13 & 14: Location & Maps**
- [ ] `FusedLocationProviderClient` from `com.google.android.gms:play-services-location`.
- [ ] `LocationRequest` with `PRIORITY_HIGH_ACCURACY`.
- [ ] `ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION` permission.
- [ ] Google Maps requires API key in Manifest.
